programme.title: Freaky
programme.desc: When a serial killers mystical dagger causes him to magically switch bodies with a 17-year-old girl, the frightened teen learns she has just 24 hours to get her identity back before she looks like a middle-aged maniac forever.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: More curious couples sex-periment with opening up their relationships. Connor and Kirstie explore having a threesome. Chanice and Richardo face an unexpected issue.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, owner Alan Johnson and his best mate and professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to be crowned best value BandB of the week.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This visit is to the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, owned by Nick and Gina Mead.The afternoon entertainment is some freezing competition out on the locks.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the competition is to York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Burgius hopes his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition is at Heyford House in Lower Heyford, Oxfordshire. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock sees as a perfect opportunity to settle some scores with Dave and Alan.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers meet for the last time to thrash out their differences and find out whos won. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, a hot chocolate storm, and accusations of over-enthusiastic hosting.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This weeks competition kicks off in Falkirk, with mum and daughter Melissa and Fiona hosting at their cosy pub. Will a fridge-less room be their downfall?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Cliff and Monikas place in the countryside near Glasgow, one BandBer learns that maybe too much nature can be a bad thing in their jungle-themed room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brothers John and Andrew host the weeks third visit, at the glampsite on their family farm in Dumfries and Galloway, where things get heated with saunas and hot tubs.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sue hosts the weeks final visit, at her Victorian townhouse BandB in Penrith, where a marmalade tasting is followed by some bittersweet discussion of payment day.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, a number of BandBers are certain that their competitors have been doing some tit-for-tat marking. When the dust settles, wholl win the week?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. Henry finds a tractor which hasnt run for years.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil catches up with Chloe, who wanted to fly the nest to be near the cafes and buzz of trendy Caversham, and Ella and Grant, who needed something spectacular to tempt Grant out of his mums house.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A lifeboat is converted into a yellow submarine camping pod, a mum and three daughters create a charming straw bale house, and George enjoys an oriental delight on the Isle of Wight.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps Don and Margaret search for a bolthole on Portugals western Algarve to escape the British winters. Can they find their dream holiday home within their £300k budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps Robert and Thomas find their perfect holiday home in and around La Mata in south east Spain. With a maximum budget of £82k, can they find their dream pad?

programme.title: Milano Cortina 2026 Paralympics LIVE
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey, Sean Rose and Alice Tai present live coverage of the spectacular closing ceremony.

programme.title: Stillwater
programme.desc: An American oil-rig roughneck travels to Marseille, France, to visit his estranged daughter, in prison for a murder she claims she didnt commit. Confronted with language barriers, cultural differences and a complicated legal system, he soon builds a new life for himself as he makes it his personal mission to exonerate her.

programme.title: Mothering Sunday
programme.desc: The story takes place on Mothers Day in 1924. Fir Mr. and Mrs. Evan give their housekeeper, Jane Fairchild (Odessa Young), the day off, as the couple are set to go to their neighbours house to celebrate his engagement. The truth is Jane and the neighbour, Paul, have been having an affair for many years.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder faces his feelings about the disappearance of his sister after a girl in Iowa mysteriously vanishes.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Shot over three months, this documentary series follows the snowplough drivers and patrols who work around the clock to make sure the UK does not grind to a halt when winter weather hits. In the Highlands, snowplough teams battle blizzards, ice, flooding and landslides to keep the roads open, whilst across the UK patrols fight snow, gales and icy conditions to reach motorists stranded in the cold.

programme.title: The Alamo
programme.desc: Star-studded saga of the 1836 defence of the Alamo, when a 187-strong garrison including William Travis and Jim Bowie fought to save Texas from Santa Annas Mexican army for 13 days. The rivalry of the two leaders was tempered by the arrival of Davy Crockett, who brought his Tennessee volunteers to fight alongside them.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: When Craigs little brother Brent cuts corners to get extra wood off the mountain, a mistake shuts the entire operation down. With the business hanging in the balance, Craig needs to decide if he can bail Brent out.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craigs newest driver must prove himself on Mud Mountain; facing ice, muck and massive pressure, Theron tries to stay ahead of the fleet, only to be stopped by someone elses mistake.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Things go south when Craigs brother Brent borrows a logging machine, leading to a risky mountainside recovery. Veteran log hauler Dan Millar battles rough roads and torn up tyres to make it to the mill.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Trouble on the traction line leaves Craig with endless breakdowns. A driver fights a chronic back injury to keep the trucks moving and a log hauler goes off the road forcing an all-night rescue.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britains roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Dirty Home Rescue

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi boasts to Mackenzie about his interview for ED doctor at Northern Districts - the positions as good as his.

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Nush receives intel about two men who are acting suspiciously. Guards Kasey and Vaz are dispatched to keep eyes on them. As the duo leave the shop, they set off the alarm and the chase is on.

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law
programme.desc: In West Orchards, guards Paige and Mark have eyes on a gang acting suspiciously in one of the centres premium stores. With Paige and Mark being hopelessly outnumbered, guards Lewis and Keith arrive as backup.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops clamp down on young drivers causing danger on north Yorkshires roads. In the Yorkshire Dales, the traffic cops hunt down a young man believed to be drug driving and whos failed to stop in a suspected stolen car.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A prolific car thief, with a string of convictions, crashes and rolls a stolen car at over 100mph. In Harrogate, the hunt for a disqualified driver leads to a high-speed car chase.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Near York, a young driver in a stolen car is on a suspected high behind the wheel. The police must make a move before the reckless man can join a busy motorway and cause more mayhem.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Drink-driving, drug use and atrocious weather all contribute to trouble for the traffic police as they investigate cases including a driver who is spotted taking drugs whilst behind the wheel of a car transporter.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team tackle a lairy drunk, a runaway knifeman, a suspected drug dealer, an overturned trailer full of cows and a ruptured gas main.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A stick of dynamite is brought into Carlisles main police station, a gun is spotted in a city-centre bar and Duncan receives a shock when a cyclist who has been crushed and trapped under a car turns out to be someone he knows.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Cumbrias elite interceptor teams are called out to deal with a suspect gunman, a pedestrian under a car and to destroy a cannabis farm, and a Reliant Robin is bid a fiery farewell.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A serial offender gives officers the runaround, a high-speed pursuit comes to a painful finish, the occupants of a 4x4 need rescuing after overturning on treacherous ice, and the interceptors are called on a mucky mercy mission after two of their own get caught in a sticky situation.

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank explores the mysteries and secrets of the bridges that have made London what it is. He uncovers stories of bronze-age relics emerging from the Vauxhall shore, of why London Bridge was falling down, of midnight corpses splashing beneath Waterloo Bridge, and above all, of the sublime ambition of Londons bridgebuilders themselves.

programme.title: Janet Suzman Remembers... Hedda Gabler
programme.desc: Renowned actress Dame Janet Suzman reflects on her acclaimed 1972 portrayal of Ibsens formidable heroine Hedda Gabler.

programme.title: Ibsen
programme.desc: The 1972 adaption of Henrik Ibsens play about Hedda Gabler, who is newly wed and feeling trapped and dangerous.

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: How does connecting with the images and sounds of the natural world help us gain a greater sense of ease, perspective and connection? This first episode is about breathing. By immersing ourselves in images of jellyfish floating, elephants swimming and lemurs swinging through the rainforest, we learn to focus on our breathing and are reminded that we are not separate from the world around us. What is the relationship between each breath and mindfulness, and why is breathing so important.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow visits Thirlestane Castle in the Scottish Borders, where treasures include a Rod Stewart gold disc, a silver chalice thought to have been found in a potato field, and a gold, enamelled snuffbox gifted by a Russian tsar. Katherine Wright and Charlotte Riordan discover two artworks featuring Thirlestane Castle itself - a 19th-century embroidered sampler and a rare watercolour by Borders artist Tom Scott.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary Bennet longs to shine as brightly as her sisters but often feels overlooked. She hopes to finally win her familys approval at the upcoming Meryton assembly.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges, isolating Carey even further from those around her. With pressure building to find the assassin, Careys assertion that SO15 is compromised gains little traction. Convinced that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment, Carey takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected...

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

Katherine Wright and Charlotte Riordan discover two artworks featuring Thirlestane Castle itself - a 19th-century embroidered sampler and a rare watercolour by Borders artist Tom Scott.

Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected...

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After being invited to a bar crawl on Khaosan Road, Zara gets under the skin of Bangkoks notorious red light district, Soi Cowboy - but filming is swiftly shut down by local bar owners and the police. When Zara meets Mike Yu, a young British expat living a life of luxury in the Thai capital, she realises theres more to the city than meets the eye. Meanwhile, a visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law in Thailand.

programme.title: New York, New York
programme.desc: Martin Scorseses romantic post-war musical film celebrates the glorious days of New Yorks big band era and centres on a dedicated musician who finds his devotion torn between his music and his songstress-wife.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Twelve months after exposing Correction in the most dramatic circumstances, whistleblower Rachel Carey is back, ready to launch Operation Veritas, a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing. That is until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: Todays guide is 45-year-old executive coach, and all-round action woman, Teresa. She takes the walkers to Kielder, in the west of Northumberland, Englands largest forest with over 200 square miles of trees.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope are in the East of England following the journey of Britains sugar beet, 100 years after the first production of home-grown sugar.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBCs Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and guide dog Major explore Bathgate, a town with an industrial past. Ian helps at a butchers shop, climbs a hill to find some Neolithic remains, visits a historical theatre, meets the local brass band and some local film-makers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent central England. Film producer Alison Owen helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: Lover Come Back
programme.desc: An ambitious advertising executive is determined to win an account from her rival - not knowing that he has fabricated a non-existent product.

programme.title: Pillow Talk
programme.desc: Interior designer Jan loathes playboy Brad because he is always hogging the line in his attempts to seduce women. He in turn thinks she is a strident harridan who sabotages his pick-up lines. However, they then meet without knowing each others identity - and set off sparks of a different kind.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul discovers the architectural history of Hopetoun House and why it is such a unique treasure in the Scottish countryside.

programme.title: How the West Was Won
programme.desc: An epic western about three generations of a pioneer family, showing how its fortunes fluctuated during the dynamic westward expansion of America during the 19th century. Three top directors each tackle separate episodes of the story.

programme.title: Earths Great Rivers
programme.desc: The extraordinary wildlife, landscapes and people of the Amazon, the worlds greatest river. From boiling streams and transparent lagoons, to pink river dolphins and riotous river carnivals, its great size hides spectacular secrets.

programme.desc: All the talking points and in-depth analysis from the final round of the 2026 Six Nations.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A quad bike accident reveals the dangers of hill farming.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Under attack from male lions, the mums with young cubs search for the rest of the pride; cheetah cubs face danger during a vital lesson in survival.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a teenager is left fighting for his life after a stabbing in a car park, forensics must prove whether two brothers arrested nearby are the attackers or victims.

programme.title: The Martian
programme.desc: After a fierce storm Astronaut Mark Watney is presumed dead and is left behind by his crew during a manned mission to Mars, but Watney has survived and finds himself stranded and alone on the hostile planet.

programme.title: Zero
programme.desc: In 2045, all adults have fled to the mainland. When the final ferry has sailed, three siblings are left stranded - but the Gateway offers food, order and a new way of life.

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Strange things are happening in Marvin Gardens, and theyre starting to attract unwanted attention. Its time for Kacey and Michael to exhume the prophets from their pile of manure.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Features a studio audience quizzing top politicians on the events of the day. The panel surrounding presenter Fiona Bruce is composed of at least one member of the three major parties.

programme.title: UFO: POV

Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebes, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachels new boss and colleague both smoke. When Rachel discovers that they make business decisions without her in the smoking room, she pretends to be a serious smoker so she can be part of the discussion. Meanwhile, Rosss son and Joey audition for the same commercial, but only one of them can get the part.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Cant Flirt
programme.desc: Ross really likes the pretty pizza-delivery woman, but his flirting technique is awful. Meanwhile, a nasty surprise awaits Joey when he tells everyone about his big television appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ride Along
programme.desc: The gang persuade Phoebes policeman boyfriend to let them ride in his police car. The friends enjoy the experience, until a gunshot rings out. Elsewhere, Rachel overhears a telephone message for Ross from his second ex-wife.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Gary confides in Monica that he intends to ask Phoebe to move in with him, Monica immediately breaks her promise to him and tells Phoebe, who worries that it is too early in the relationship. She persuades Chandler to talk to Gary, hoping that Chandlers fear of commitment will rub off on him, but Garys enthusiasm overwhelms even Chandler.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joeys Big Break
programme.desc: Joeys first major film role leads to an unexpected experience in Las Vegas. Meanwhile, Phoebe cant quite remember why she hates Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang travel to Las Vegas, Nevada, where more than one surprise awaits them. After Ross misinterprets Rachels actions, a war of public humiliation results.

programme.title: Beethovens 2nd
programme.desc: Comedic sequel reuniting the grown-up St Bernard Beethoven and his reluctant owner, George Newton, and family. Beethoven discovers love as he turns his attention to Missy, who belongs to the heartless Regina, and soon four puppies arrive in the world. While the little dogs cause havoc in the Newton household, Regina makes plans to steal them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang are having fun in Las Vegas, where Monica and Chandler get lucky, Rachel makes Ross regret playing a prank on her, and Joey schemes for fame and fortune.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Ross cant figure out if theyre legally married, and Monica and Chandler search for matrimonial signs.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel
programme.desc: Joey is upset to learn that Chandler plans to move out to live with Monica, but Rachel seems strangely unaffected by the news. Meanwhile, Phoebe pleads with Ross to either get an annulment or admit that he still loves Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler disagree over how to redecorate Rachels old room, and despite Phoebes admonitions, Ross makes a drastic decision about Rachel.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Loses His Insurance
programme.desc: After learning that his health insurance has lapsed because he has not worked enough as an actor, Joey suffers a painful hernia, and Rachel finally finds out that Ross never annulled their marriage.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel forces Ross to terminate their marriage, Phoebes first triplet babysitting experience proves stressful, and Joey pretends to own a German sports car.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One on the Last Night
programme.desc: Its the last night before Chandler moves into Monicas apartment, leaving Joey alone and prompting Rachel to make plans to move in with Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes lack of coordination while jogging embarrasses Rachel, and Joey gets a sexy new roommate.

programme.title: What Women Want
programme.desc: A chauvinistic advertising executive acquires the ability to hear what women are thinking. He attempts to use this power to bring about the fall of his female boss, but as he uses this inside knowledge to outwit his superior, the bachelor begins to fall in love with her, and so finds himself feeling more than a little guilty when his plan to have her sacked looks like it may come to fruition.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Treasure Quest: Snake Island
programme.desc: According to a legend that dates back to the 16th century, a cache of Incan gold worth hundreds of millions of dollars is hidden off the coast of southeastern Brazil. Its known as the `Treasure of the Trinity, and its eluded all who have sought it for 500 years. But a modern-day team of explorers follows a radical new theory to uncover its whereabouts: a remote, treacherous island where thousands of deadly vipers roam. Cork Graham leads the expedition to Queimada Grande - known to locals as Snake Island - armed with advanced technology to unearth complex clues and decipher puzzles that have protected the treasure for centuries. This Discovery series takes viewers along on the epic journey to one of the most dangerous places on Earth.

programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show. Rob Beckett, Paisley Billings and Josh Jones pit their wits against Katherine Ryan, Tom Read Wilson and Kiri Pritchard-McLean.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson thrash out the hot stories of the week and are joined by actor and comedian Johnny Vegas and Joe Lycett.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian and presenter Jimmy Carr welcomes team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson who thrash out the hot stories of the week with singer Olly Murs, former member of the Pussycat Dolls Kimberly Wyatt, and comedians Matthew Crosby and Russell Peters.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Bernard gets locked out of the shop with Manny still inside. Neither of them can operate the new security system.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Lorne Abony is the CEO and Chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, one of the fastest growing companies in America. He adopts a disguise and goes undercover in his own business to find out how efficiently it is functioning.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jim Rogers from Billings, Montana, is the Chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America (KOA). Jim goes undercover in his own business disguised as an accountant who has recently been made redundant.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the Lake District, first hosts Paul and Glenda welcome self-confessed gadget geek David and his partner Jane, and outdoorsy twenty-somethings Matt and Polly, to their static caravan.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Kent, Stephanie and Ians home, a tiny version of the Palace of Versailles, amazes their guests. Is their food as impressive? Later in the week, theres dressing up and stripping off.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: A single mother wants to move into a bigger property with her four boys - but Drew and Jonathan will need to spruce up her well-worn abode first.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Nathan loves music and Stoke-on-Trent, so when bride-to-be Beccie gives him free rein to plan their wedding, he honours them with nuptials based on Northern Soul.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One on a Thai Farm
programme.desc: Hopeless romantic Gavin plans a belated hilltop proposal for fiancee Sophie, as well as nuptials with a combined theme of farmyard and Thai. He hopes his plans are not over complicated and he is not aiming too high.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Anthony Wedo, president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover posing as a construction worker taking part in a reality TV competition to win enough money to start up his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits Applegate, Oregon, to try to rescue the Applegate River Lodge, which is around a million dollars in debt. Gordon questions Applegate owner Richard Davis interest in smoking cannabis.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: After emigrating to Canada from India five years prior, a couple hope to reunite their extended family under one big roof.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Pasadena, California to visit a Jamaican restaurant called Cafe Kingston. There, Gordon finds Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works as a radiotherapist in a clinic next door to the restaurant. She is determined to use hospital techniques in her kitchen and is constantly sealing everything into plastic bags. Dr Morris is as stubborn as she is proud. Can Gordon help?

programme.title: Hollyoaks: Omnibus
programme.desc: A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones. And a residents romantic hopes end in betrayal.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claires new positivity gets her in trouble when it comes to Lukes relationship. Its Phil who gets the shock when he surprises Alex at school.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay courts an Instagram celeb as he tries to get a new venture off the ground. Haley and Dylans secret plan to elope doesnt exactly go smoothly.

programme.title: My Spy
programme.desc: Nine-year-old Sophie catches JJ, a hardened CIA operative, spying on her family during a routine surveillance operation. In exchange for not blowing his cover, JJ begrudgingly agrees to show the precocious girl how to become a spy. What at first seems like an easy task soon turns into a battle of wits as Sophie proves you dont need much experience to outsmart a seasoned agent.

programme.desc: Robert McCalls mysterious past cuts especially close to home when thugs kill Susan Plummer, his best friend and former colleague. Now out for revenge, McCall must take on a crew of highly trained assassins who will stop at nothing to destroy him.

programme.title: The Infernal Machine
programme.desc: Reclusive and controversial author Bruce Cogburn is drawn out of hiding when he begins to receive endless letters from an obsessive fan. What ensues is a dangerous labyrinth as Bruce searches for the person behind the cryptic messages.

programme.title: The Darkest Minds
programme.desc: When teens mysteriously develop powerful new abilities, they are declared a threat by the government and detained. One of the most powerful young people, Ruby, escapes from her camp and joins a group of runaways who are seeking a safe haven. Betrayed by the adults in power, this newfound family soon realises that running is not enough -- they must use their collective powers to wage a resistance and take back their future.

programme.desc: Tarragon potato salad; chunky blue cheese dressing; vegetable coleslaw; guacamole.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emilys David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britains food heroes and shes using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: War on the two fronts caught up with Hitler leaving him on the run in mid 1943.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: The Rise of Hitler
programme.desc: Exploring the socio-political climate of post-WWI Germany, where the Weimar Republic grappled with the aftermath of war, economic instability and political uncertainty.

programme.title: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: The triumphs of the Prime Minister who led England through its darkest hour and on to victory.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: Operation Oblivion
programme.desc: In the darkest days of World War II in the Pacific, an elite British military team sought to facilitate espionage and sabotage behind enemy lines. This now famous group, the British Special Operations Executive or MI6, faced a critical obstacle.

programme.title: Benito Mussolini: Italys Greatest Evil
programme.desc: Benito Amilcare Andrea Mussolini was an Italian dictator and journalist who founded and led the National Fascist Party. Learn of how he went from a shy boy in a small town in the province of Forlì in Romagna to arguably Italys greatest evil.

programme.title: Churchill: The Road to Victory
programme.desc: Winston Churchill served as the Prime Minister of Great Britain in arguably some of the most trying times of modern history. After Chamberlains resignation in 1940, Churchill was appointed during World War II and ultimately led the country down the road to victory.

programme.title: May 1940: Churchills Finest Hour
programme.desc: For the 80th anniversary of Britains darkest hour Dan Snow visits the House of Commons to learn about the speeches that turned the tide of WWII.

programme.title: Secrets of the Churchill War Rooms
programme.desc: In this film Dan Snow pays a visit to the Churchill War Rooms, walking in the footsteps of the great man.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: The triumphs of the Prime Minister who led England through its darkest hour and on to victory.

programme.title: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: The triumphs of the Prime Minister who led England through its darkest hour and on to victory.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the citys story.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Elizabethan Plymouth, exploring the story of Englands `Golden Age, from Sir Francis Drake to the Spanish Armada, piracy, theft, and the slave trade.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Secrets Of The Devils Bible
programme.desc: "The Codex Gigas," also known as the "Devils Bible," is a giant medieval tome that has evoked fear for centuries and believed to contain a malevolent power. Experts investigate its legendary secrets and uncover the mystery behind its author.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: After seizing power in 1933, Adolf Hitler began systematically transforming a peaceful German population into a ruthless war machine. Experts investigate the innovative methods used by the Nazis to indoctrinate and control the will of its citizens.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Quest To Find The Tomb Of Mary Magdalene
programme.desc: Mary Magdalene played a pivotal role in early Christianity and witnessed the execution and resurrection of Jesus Christ. Experts investigate the truth behind her mysterious disappearance and embark on a quest to locate her undiscovered remains.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Mysteries of the Worlds Oldest Temple
programme.desc: The ancient site of Gobekli Tepe is the oldest building complex ever discovered and continues to baffle archaeologists today. Now, experts investigate new theories behind its enigmatic construction as a possible commemoration to a cataclysmic event.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: A subterranean Neolithic temple discovered on the island of Malta contains the skeletal remains of thousands of humans. Experts use forensic archaeology to investigate elongated skulls that may unlock the secrets of an advanced, lost civilisation.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: Christ and Crucifixion: The Real Evidence
programme.desc: The crucifixion of Jesus Christ has long been debated by historians due to scarce archaeological evidence of the event. Experts explore theories that may reveal the truth behind the traditional yet controversial story of his monumental execution.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Search For King Solomons Mines
programme.desc: The location of King Solomons Mines has baffled historians for centuries and continues to lure treasure hunters today. Archaeologists investigate evidence that may uncover the unlikely truth behind this source of untold riches.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Tunstall in Shropshire and Prescott in Liverpool and a large flat in Dagenham are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion have a look round before hearing what the new owners have planned.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are todays investors. Theyve bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts rolls back the clock nearly 12 years to catch up on an ambitious development of a former church in Preston on the Hill in Cheshire, while Lucy Alexander looks round two very different terraced houses in Stoney Stratford, near Milton Keynes, and Streatham in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s terrace in Croydon, a semi in Wolstanton destined to be a family home and the only house on a street in Moorside in Oldham are all under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion visit the properties and meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 20-year-old first-time developer, a banker turned developer and a builder who wants to create a new home for his soon-to-be-wife all buy properties at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion are in Chesterton, Hornchurch and Sudbrook to hear their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: The Futos are a close-knit family of five who film their lives over many decades; four of them are found brutally murdered and the slayings uncover a terrible secret.

programme.desc: When wealthy bachelor Steve Clayton meets homely VA nurse Lana Walsh, its love at first sight. But the devoted couple arent immune to lifes tragedies, and when an untimely death raises questions, an unsettling and devastating secret is revealed.

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Joseph Green Brown was on death row for 13 years before his convictions were overturned. Decades later, he would be charged for the murder of his wife.

programme.desc: When Holly Cantrell never returns home after work, her three sons are distraught. It soon comes to light that Holly harboured a web of secrets that may have led to her disappearance.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: Ali Lowizter, 16, vanishes after getting off her school bus. The investigation stalls for two years until a tip comes in claiming Lowizter could have been trafficked, leading to a SWAT team raid on a house of prostitution.

programme.title: Fletchers Family Farm
programme.desc: As winter approaches on the farm, so does the Fletchers biggest event of the year in the form of a six-day-long Christmas Celebrations gala that welcomes families to the farm for festive fun - with preparations under way, friends and family are called in to lend a hand while the children all muck in too, with Milo turning his hand to making a sign for Santas grotto and helping to pack up blankets and gin that are to be delivered ahead of the big day.

From a half-built castle folly, to a playful water feature and plant-petting corner, filled with sensory plants, this garden has everything to delight a young girl and her family.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Cheshire
programme.desc: Nicole finally turns 50 and Joe and Seema plan a surprise party in her honour - however, friendships are fragile between the Housewives after Lauren stormed out at Paiges motherhood event and Joe must now round up the ladies for some fun while preventing the hoedown from becoming a showdown.

programme.sub-title: One if by Clam, Two if by Sea

programme.desc: Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an attempt is made to steal a headless, thirty-year-old skeleton from an archaeological dig, Wycliffe and the team discover an estate steeped in archaic values.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When surfer Anne Carter is discovered in a deep coma on the beach, evidence points to an accident or a possible suicide attempt, but as Wycliffe and the team investigate, they discover a plot far more sinister.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Over an August Bank Holiday, an empty Oxford college is the venue for a weekend attended by professional quiz contestants. Lewis and Hathaway are called in when outstanding competitor Ethan Croft is found floating dead in the college fountain. They learn that Croft was an outrageous flirt with a number of secrets, and the pair race to uncover the killer before the gathering breaks up.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Its the summer of 1971. Endeavour and the team are called to investigate the murder of a cab driver found dead in his taxi on the outskirts of Oxford. With a recent string of assaults on cab drivers, the team worry that assault has escalated to murder and the population of Oxfordshire is at this killers mercy. Meanwhile, Endeavour receives a guest at home who reminds him of a past hed sooner forget.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa go out on their first date and she seizes the opportunity for a romantic kiss, but the clumsy doctor manages to ruin the moment with an inappropriate remark. Meanwhile, Louisas friend Hollys condition takes a turn for the worse when she has an allergic reaction to morphine, and Martin has to act quickly to save her life. Louisa is full of admiration for him - and he surprises her by proposing.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with the news of Martin and Louisas engagement - but Aunt Joan is disappointed that she has to hear about the impending nuptials from the village postman rather than her own nephew. Elsewhere, Beth Sawles wound isnt healing and Martin discovers that her sister has been treating her with a homemade potion - which proves to be the cause of an outbreak of aspergillosis in the village.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: As Laura and Rosemary visit a prep school to investigate dying roses, they are caught up in a strange ritual involving a member of staff dressed as a stag. The geography master is then found in the garden with a harpoon through his chest.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura enter the cut-throat world of TV garden makeovers when they help a presenter with her new series. When Rosemary is injured in a suspicious accident, Laura and the presenter team up to investigate further.

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.desc: A wedding in Bath ends in tragedy when the vicars lifeless body is discovered in the sacristy; McDonalds suspicions centre on the girlfriend, Lotty, her father and her sisters, who had a close relationship with the priest.

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: When a young blonde woman is found strangled in the library of Gossington Hall, Dolly Bantry quickly calls in her old friend Miss Marple to help her with a bit of sleuthing. They soon discover that the dead woman was a dancer from the glamorous Majestic Hotel and decide to check themselves in there in order to observe the guests and solve the murder.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When one of the worlds rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death. Barnaby and Troy must find both the flower and the person prepared to commit murder in order to get their hands on it.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: With her caustic wit and singular charm, DCI Vera Stanhope and her trusted right-hand man face a series of captivating murder mysteries set against the breathtaking Northumberland landscape.

programme.title: After the Flood
programme.desc: Captivating mystery thriller. Newly-promoted to the murder team, Detective Jo Marshall investigates a perplexing double murder while working in secret with ex-husband Pat to stop the corrupt Sergeant Mackie.

programme.title: After the Flood
programme.desc: Jo and Sam struggle to link Xav Palmer to Todd Drake.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The busmen decide to get fit after reading an article which states that drivers have a shorter life expectancy than conductors.

programme.desc: Peppa and George check out Grandpas new Bug Hotel.

programme.title: Pet Shop Boys - Rock Legends
programme.desc: Neil Tennant and Chris Lowe met in a record store in West London. Although they came from different backgrounds they bonded over a love of electronic music. Their debut album Please was released in 1986, it entered the top 10 around the world.

programme.title: LL Cool J - Countdown Concert

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: enry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 70s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The boys are coming to the end of their journey as they head to Wales where Henry converts a Royal Enfield into a classic cafe racer, and Simon gets an 80s pinball machine back to its best.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Road Tripping favourites Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their antiques hunt in the bucolic Cotswold town of Stow-On-The-Wold. Theyre pointing their 1960s classic car to Wales, via Gloucestershire in the hunt for profit making collectibles.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie and Izzie continue their B Road romp, pointing their classic car to Surrey. Gemmologist Izzie finds a piece of jewellery that causes quite a stir at auction. And Charlies passion for furniture in all shades of brown sees him uncover a very rare table that sparks a frenzy of bids at the Dorking auction.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer find themselves weaving their classic car through the b-roads of the North Wessex Downs. Charlie decides to play Izzie at her own game and hunts for `sparkly antiques. However, hes soon distracted by an exquisite Edwardian box that holds a surprise.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After a quick lesson in how to identify different wood, Izzie gambles on some brown furniture. Charlie,meanwhile, ends up in a shops storeroom and uncovers an antique hes never before seen but is confident will excite the bidders of Sidmouth.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Captain Izzie Balmer and First Mate Charlie Ross are sailing to the Isle of Wight for their final leg of this Road Trip. Charlie rips his trousers while on horseback, and Izzie ditches the classic car in favour of a speedboat.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First up, the team bring a well-loved Indian guitar back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie is challenged with restoring a vintage RAF-issued flying jacket.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Won in 1955, it commemorates a young girl who turned her back on singing when she started a family. With one handle missing and the silver plate tarnished and dented, the trophy is in bad shape.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal expert Dominic restores a 1940s snail riding toy. Plus, silversmith Brenton works on a treasured trophy won in a 1949 ballroom dancing competition.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus is in Wallsend, Newcastle, to clear out a home that is full of books. He takes interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving-cup.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team are called out to peoples homes with a mission to sift through a lifetime of possessions and find forgotten treasures that can be sold at auction.

programme.title: Celebrity Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team help celebrities clear their homes of clutter. They look for hidden gems to be auctioned off for profit.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal maestro Dominic Chinea is blown away by a prewar ride-on toy aeroplane that has been grounded for decades. Owner Charlotte is hoping to restore her grandpas vintage toy for her own daughter Phoebe to enjoy. Rusty and in pieces, getting the plane fit for piloting again is going to be a mammoth job for Dom.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First to arrive is Tom Wenham, with a pair of running shoes used by his grandma when she represented Great Britain at the Berlin Olympics in 1936. programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: When firefighters in suburban Indianapolis are called to a house fire, what they find inside shocks the entire community, a respected minister and his wife have been murdered with an ax. An exhaustive investigation leads authorities to a teenage church member and his accomplice.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: A string of rapes spanning two decades goes unsolved in Jacksonville, Florida, until Detective Diana Clarke finds a link among the crimes and discovers that a serial rapist is terrorizing the community. After a suspect is finally arrested, Detective Clarke teams up with Prosecutor Libby Senterfitt to track down the rapists victims and convince them to testify.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: A robbery and double homicide at a grocery store in Raleigh, North Carolina shocks the community. With little evidence left behind, and no eyewitnesses, the investigation stalls. Then a tip to authorities implicates a store employee in another robbery and leads Prosecutor Evelyn Hill to believe that that employee, and his brother, committed the murders.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: A church shootout in northern Virginia leads authorities to an ex-FBI agents home. When an unwitting accomplice comes forward the mans complex plot to get revenge on his estranged wife becomes clear.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: A double murder of young women startles residents in a California community.

programme.title: Trevor McDonald and the Killer Nurse
programme.desc: Trevor McDonald investigates the case of serial child killer Beverley Allitt and reflects on how it was reported at the time. A combination of archive from the time, interviews with victims and expert analysis will piece together this tragic and shocking story.

programme.title: The Perfect Victim
programme.desc: Three women spend more than 30 years in prison for killing their abusive husbands. They fight the system that incarcerated them when a new law is passed and their cases are reheard.

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Following a team of experts as they investigate whether evidence exists that could challenge the conviction of a man in prison for a murder he denies. Every year, over 500 people in the UK are convicted of murder. A guilty verdict can only be challenged if new evidence comes to light. Inside Justice is a specialist charity led by Louise Shorter, supported by a panel of criminal lawyers, ex-police officers and forensics experts who offer assistance to prisoners who are protesting their innocence.

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Louise investigates a case, looking for evidence that could present grounds to bring an appeal, and ultimately overturn the conviction of a Southampton man in prison for the murder of his secret lover.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: The chilling secrets of the Mafias most notorious kingpins are exposed in this compelling series. Mobster Sam Giancana was associated with the Kennedys, Frank Sinatra and the CIA.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Lucky Lucianos crimes with the Mafia, including bribing high-ranking officials, got him 50 years in jail. However, when WWII broke out, the USA needed Lucianos help.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on notorious mobsters. Joe Colombo loved fame and money and took on the FBI in the 70s, with his Italian-American Civil Rights League campaigning against harassment.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Ambitious John Gotti became the boss of the Gambino family by ordering the death of the groups former head and earned the nickname Teflon Don after three acquittals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Larry Horn believed his alibi was perfect, but agents traced his calls and located the man he hired to kill his family.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The head of the Gambino crime family in New York assassinated his boss Paul Castellano to become the new don, but was eventually convicted by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Bureaus hunt for a police killer in Washington DC in 1995.

programme.title: The Life and Death of Tupac Shakur
programme.desc: Seen as a poet whose tales of urban alienation captivated young people of all races and backgrounds, Tupac Shakur sets the stage for the current and continuing evolution of the hip-hop phenomenon.

programme.title: Killing John F. Kennedy
programme.desc: Chronicling President John F. Kennedys life and career, and probing conspiracy theories surrounding his assassination in Dallas Nov. 22, 1963.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Ex-FBI agent Joe Pistone, who infiltrated the Mafia for six years as mobster `Donnie Brasco, reveals how his undercover work led to over 100 convictions.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Financier Allen Dorfman used union pension funds to bankroll the Mafia and the FBI quickly realised they could bankrupt the Mob by pursuing Dorfman.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Joe Valachi was the first Mafia member to break the Mobs code of silence and turn public informer, revealing the term `Cosa Nostra to the world.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: The death of Peppino Impastato in 1978 was dismissed by police as a random act. But he was an anti-Mafia activist. Were the people behind his death sheltered by the Mafia?

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: The chilling secrets of the Mafias most notorious kingpins are exposed in this compelling series. Mobster Sam Giancana was associated with the Kennedys, Frank Sinatra and the CIA.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
